title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to cloud-based infrastructure, potentially resulting in data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread propagation and supply chain attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA), access controls, and regular audits.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor for suspicious activity in cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups are performed and stored offline, and test recovery procedures to minimize downtime in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in EFM ipTIME A8004T 14.18.2. This vulnerability...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in EFM ipTIME A8004T 14.18.2. This vulnerability affects the function formWifiBasicSet of the file /goform/WifiBasicSet. The manipulation of the argument security_5g leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in EFM ipTIME A8004T 14.18.2 allows remote attackers to exploit a stack-based buffer overflow via the security_5g parameter, potentially leading to unauthorized access, denial of service, or remote code execution. The exploit has been publicly disclosed, increasing the likelihood of malicious use.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable remote access to the affected device until a patch is available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems.
Monitor for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection systems to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: In libexpat before 2.8.1, the computational complexity of attribute name collision checks allows...
description: In libexpat before 2.8.1, the computational complexity of attribute name collision checks allows a denial of service via moderately sized crafted XML input.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in libexpat before version 2.8.1 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (DoS) by exploiting the computational complexity of attribute name collision checks using crafted XML input. This could lead to resource exhaustion on affected systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade libexpat to version 2.8.1 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and rate-limiting mechanisms for XML processing to reduce the risk of exploitation.
Monitor systems for unusual resource consumption patterns that may indicate an attempted DoS attack.

Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit an SQL injection flaw in the /deleteorder.php file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or database manipulation. This could compromise sensitive customer and order data.
recommended_actions: Apply input validation and parameterized queries to sanitize user inputs in the /deleteorder.php file.
Update the CodeAstro Online Catering Ordering System to the latest patched version, if available.
Monitor logs for unusual database activity and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-5337 - During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or
description: During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or higher are able to perform an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attack. This vulnerability exists because the Frontend File Manager Plugin WordPress plugin through 23.6 does not properly validate user authorization for the requested uploaded file when processing download requests. By modifying the value of the 'file_id' parameter in the download endpoint (e.g., http://localhost/?do=wpfm_download&file_id=40&nm_file_nonce=a36fb893f1), an attacker can access files belonging to other users, including privileged users such as administrators. This allows unauthorized access/read to sensitive data stored  within the application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5337.

title: CVE-2026-7681 - A security vulnerability has been detected in jsbroks COCO Annotator up to 0.11.1. Affected by this
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in jsbroks COCO Annotator up to 0.11.1. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file backend/webserver/api/datasets.py of the component Dataset API. The manipulation of the argument DatasetId leads to authorization bypass. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in jsbroks COCO Annotator up to version 0.11.1 allows for an authorization bypass via manipulation of the DatasetId parameter in the Dataset API. This could enable unauthorized users to access or manipulate sensitive dataset information, potentially leading to data breaches, loss of data integrity, or unauthorized data exposure.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade jsbroks COCO Annotator to a patched version or implement a workaround to secure the Dataset API if no patch is available.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for the DatasetId parameter to prevent unauthorized manipulation.
Restrict access to the Dataset API by enforcing authentication and authorization mechanisms, such as role-based access controls.

title: CVE-2026-5063 - The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via POST parameter key names in the submit_nex_form() function in versions up to, and including, 9.1.11 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

title: CVE-2026-40561 - Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.
description:
Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.

Starlet incorrectly prioritizes "Content-Length" over "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" when both headers are present in an HTTP request. Per RFC 7230 3.3.3, Transfer-Encoding must take precedence.

An attacker could exploit this to smuggle malicious HTTP requests via a front-end reverse proxy.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40561.
